source_jobs.title: Satellite Production Technician, Systems and Software
source_jobs.description:
Role highlights:



• Satellite Production Technician, Systems and Software
• Location: Espoo, Finland
• Department: Global Manufacturing Organisation
• Reports to: Tejpalsinh Solanki
• Employment type: Permanent
• Workplace model: Office
• Employment is subject to applicable security screening (incl. SUPO, where required)

 

Why this role matters:



As a satellite production systems technician, you play a vital role in ensuring that ICEYE satellite testing meets aerospace quality standards. You will perform automated verification procedures for functional, payload and Bus tests to validate performance at the subsystem and system levels. You will conduct operational simulations (MockOps) to verify operational readiness. You will also oversee final activities, ensure configuration control and support launch campaigns.



Who We Are



ICEYE is the world leader in sovereign intelligence from space. We deliver persistent monitoring capabilities to detect and respond to changes in any location on Earth.



ICEYE owns the world's largest and most advanced SAR (synthetic aperture radar) satellite constellation. To our customers we provide intelligence with unmatched quality, latency and revisit times, in any weather, day or night. To governments who choose to operate their own constellation we provide this proven capability as a sovereign system.



ICEYE-built constellations serve customers in defence and intelligence, environmental monitoring, insurance and emergency management. We enable fast decisions that contribute to a safer future.



Founded and headquartered in Finland, ICEYE operates globally with over 1000 employees across Europe, North America, the Middle East, and Asia-Pacific.



Your day-to-day responsibilities



 - Manage schedule priorities and execute work tasks to meet production timelines.

 - Independently performs a variety of tasks according to detailed work instructions and established procedures.

 - Operate mechanical and electrical ground support equipment to ensure smooth test operations.

 - Proactive communication with team members and supervisors regarding any challenges or obstacles.

 - Troubleshoots basic technical issues and implements corrective actions under guidance.

 - Assists in training and mentoring junior technicians.

 - Maintains accurate records and documentation of work performed, including documenting results in Jira and creating NCRs when necessary.

 - Assisting in the management of activities related to satellite launch campaigns.

 - Actively contributes to continuous improvement initiatives within the team, focusing on procedures, processes, tools, and 6S.

 - Work closely with other production teams and ensure clear communication to ensure that deadlines for the satellite building phase and technical specifications are met.

 

What we’re looking for



Must haves:

 - Relevant university degree in Aeronautical engineering, Electronics, Space studies, Applied Physics or similar, or equivalent experience in a related role.

 - Hands-on experience with setting up and running tests and experiments.

 - Excellent communication skills, with particular attention to detail regarding procedures, preparation and execution.

 - Experience using lab equipment, such as power supplies, multimeters, oscilloscopes, etc.

 - Able to proficiently work in a linux environment.

 - Ability to perform tests on satellite systems using Python or Shell scripts for routine tasks.

 - Ability to read and interpret technical drawings, assembly instructions, and schematics.

 - Proficient in English, both written and spoken, to ensure clear communication in a cross-functional environment.

 

Nice to haves:

 - Experience using Atlassian Cloud (Jira, Confluence)

 - Ability to improve/and or write tests scripts in Python

 - Space background or knowledge about satellite systems

 - Skills in electronics and embedded systems

 

Application Process



• Application review by Hiring Manager
• Initial rec

source_jobs.title: Flood Data Scientist
source_jobs.description:
Description

Role highlights:

 - Flood Data Scientist

 - Location: Espoo, Finland

 - Department: Solutions

 - Reports to: Director of Product Engineering

 - Employment type: Permanent

 - Workplace model: Hybrid

 - Employment is subject to applicable security screening (incl. SUPO, where required)



Why this role matters:

ICEYE leverages proprietary satellite-derived observations and other auxiliary data to develop geospatial flood risk models, requiring strong hydrological expertise to extract reliable signals.

We need a Flood Data Scientist to bridge the gap between scientific rigor and engineering practicality. You will lead the effort to extract reliable hydrological signals from diverse, often noisy, geospatial datasets to support our expansion across the United States.

This is an outcome-oriented role: you are responsible for the quality of the features and the integrity of the labels that power our training pipelines. We expect a balance of an experimental mindset for feature discovery and a 'built-to-last' discipline for model validation. By utilizing AI-augmented workflows, you will maintain high-velocity delivery while ensuring our risk scores remain statistically meaningful and grounded in physical reality.


Who We Are

ICEYE delivers space-based intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) capabilities to governments and allied nations. This includes sovereign and turnkey ISR missions leveraging ICEYE’s world-leading synthetic aperture radar (SAR) satellite technology, as well as access to data from the world’s largest SAR satellite constellation. These capabilities enable partners to detect and respond to critical changes anywhere on Earth with unprecedented speed and accuracy – day or night and in any weather, supported by ultra high-resolution imagery and high-frequency revisits.

As a trusted partner for defense, intelligence, security, and maritime domain awareness, ICEYE’s near real-time data creates a tactical advantage for mission-critical operations. Designed for dual use, the platform also serves civil protection and commercial users for natural-catastrophe intelligence, insurance, maritime monitoring (including oil-spill detection), and finance, contributing to global security and community resilience.

ICEYE is headquartered in Finland and operates globally across Europe, North America, the Middle East, and Asia-Pacific. We have more than 900 employees, united by a shared vision: improving

Requirements


Your day-to day responsibilities

 - Feature Development: Translate environmental, terrain, and hydrological data into model-ready features grounded in physical process understanding; work closely with other hydrological subject matter experts to validate that signals reflect real-world behaviour

 - Observation Quality: Assess, extend, and improve the quality of satellite-derived observation labels, investigating noise, bias, and coverage gaps, and designing labelling approaches that reflect confidence in the data

 - Experimentation: Design and run experiments to understand what drives predictive performance; distinguish genuine signal from statistical artefact

 - Physical Interpretability: Ensure model outputs are explicable in domain terms, the kind of scrutiny that holds up in front of scientists and domain experts, not just benchmarks

 - Collaboration: Work closely with Data Engineers to define robust, scalable data and labeling workflows, and with ML Engineers to ensure features, labels, validation criteria, and model evaluation are scientifically rigorous and production-ready

What we’re looking for



Must haves:

 - Education: Master's degree or higher in hydrology, geomorphology, geoscience, environmental science, civil engineering (water resources), or related quantitative field

 - Experience: 5+ years of professional industry experience in data science with geospatial or environmental data, flood modeling, hydrology, climate risk, natural hazard assessment, or remote

The EOM is accountable for project execution and has authority over allocated resources. They propose project timelines aligned with organizational needs, request appropriate resourcing, and then own and manage the schedule across engineering teams.

For other space-system projects, the EOM proactively identifies bottlenecks, escalates challenges with recommended actions to Directors or VPs, and ensures alignment with organizational objectives.

They use available tools to motivate teams and enable project leads to work effectively. Delivering a project at the cost of team morale or collaboration is considered a failure for the role.

ICEYE is headquartered in Finland and operates globally across Europe, North America, the Middle East, and Asia-Pacific. We have more than 900 employees, united by a shared vision: improving life on Earth by becoming the global source of truth in Earth Observation.




DAY TO DAY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Lead subsystem architecture, preliminary design and performance trade-offs for HET propulsion systems, considering mission, spacecraft and operational constraints.

 - Define requirements, interfaces and verification strategies for propulsion subsystems, ensuring seamless integration with avionics, mechanical, thermal and GNC systems.

 - Perform analytical modeling and simulation of HET behavior, including plasma/beam physics, plume effects, thermal interactions and spacecraft contamination considerations.

 - Define qualification, acceptance and production-readiness criteria, driving the transition of matured subsystems into repeatable flight builds with robust test and inspection flows.

 - Monitor on-orbit propulsion performance, lead anomaly investigations and feed lessons learned back into design and production improvements.

 - Manage technical interfaces with suppliers, partners and research institutions, ensuring strong documentation and regulatory/qualification compliance.

source_jobs.title: Head of Global Manufacturing Capability & Training
source_jobs.description:
DESCRIPTION

Role highlights:

 - Head of Global Manufacturing Capability & Training

 - Location: Espoo, Finland

 - Department: Global Manufacturing Infrastructure (GMI) / Global Manufacturing Organization

 - Reports to: Head of GMI

 - Employment type: Permanent

 - Workplace model: Office / Hybrid

 - Employment is subject to applicable security screening (incl. SUPO, where required)


REQUIREMENTS

Why this role matters:

ICEYE is scaling global manufacturing and local operational capability across ICEYE sites, joint ventures, and customer-hosted locations. To deliver reliably at that scale, we need a capability system that makes teams productive fast, keeps quality high, and remains consistent as procedures, tooling, and products evolve.

As the Head of Global Manufacturing Capability & Training, you’ll build that system end-to-end: defining role-based competencies, creating world-class training paths (hands-on + blended), standing up a staged training center, and ensuring new and existing teams—across AIT, supply chain, quality, and production operations—are trained, certified, and ready to execute.

This is a leadership role for someone who can combine industrialization mindset + hardware manufacturing credibility + learning system design, and who is motivated to build something foundational for ICEYE’s next growth phase.



Who We Are

ICEYE delivers space-based intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) capabilities to governments and allied nations. This includes sovereign and turnkey ISR missions leveraging ICEYE’s world-leading synthetic aperture radar (SAR) satellite technology, as well as access to data from the world’s largest SAR satellite constellation. These capabilities enable partners to detect and respond to critical changes anywhere on Earth with unprecedented speed and accuracy – day or night and in any weather, supported by ultra high-resolution imagery and high-frequency revisits.

As a trusted partner for defense, intelligence, security, and maritime domain awareness, ICEYE’s near real-time data creates a tactical advantage for mission-critical operations. Designed for dual use, the platform also serves civil protection and commercial users for natural-catastrophe intelligence, insurance, maritime monitoring (including oil-spill detection), and finance, contributing to global security and community resilience.

ICEYE is headquartered in Finland and operates globally across Europe, North America, the Middle East, and Asia-Pacific. We have more than 900 employees, united by a shared vision: improving life on Earth by becoming the global source of truth in Earth Observation.



Your day-to-day responsibilities

 - Own the global capability model: define role taxonomy, competency frameworks, training matrices, and certification criteria across manufacturing and production support roles (AIT, supply chain/logistics, quality, stock management, etc.).

 - Reinvent the training operating model: create a modern training system (blended learning + hands-on lab + supervised practice) that measurably reduces time-to-competency and improves first-pass quality.

 - Stand up a staged training center: design and execute an MVP training center that solves immediate ramp needs, then evolve it into a scalable “academy” with clear governance, capacity planning, and content lifecycle management.

 - Enable global deployments: plan and deliver training for new sites and localization programs (ICEYE sites, JV operations, customer locations), including onboarding, role qualification, and readiness sign-off aligned to site go-live schedules.

 - Build scalable content and delivery: develop training modules, practical exercises, and assessment tools; establish a train-the-trainer network and ensure materials remain aligned with current procedures and tooling.

 - Partner across functions: work closely with production/AIT engineering, quality, supply chain, program teams, and site leadership to identify skill gaps,

notes: ICEYE is a space technology company with a strong focus on engineering and manufacturing. The company has a high volume of job postings, indicating rapid growth and expansion.
